data.body:
## Completion Verification — FAIL

Verified every AC against the implementation in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` (registry.py, orchestrator.py, passes.py, det_floor.py) and the unit tests in `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`.

### MET

**AC1 — Registry loads from prompt library; coverage guard passes.**
`registry._descriptor_from_prompt()` resolves each criterion via `prompts.get_prompt(f"plan-review-{cid}")` (registry.py:149). `check_registry_coverage()` is implemented (registry.py:297–309). `test_registry_coverage_guard_passes` and `test_criteria_load_from_the_prompt_library` cover this. `criteria_v8.json` is design-ref only; no inline prompt constants (`test_no_inline_pass_prompt_constants` guards this).

**AC2 — Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.**
`route_criteria()` applies `registry.applies()` with proportionate scrutiny (orchestrator.py:162–192); `chunk_by_facet()` packs by facet (registry.py:257–268); DET tier dispatched via `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` (orchestrator.py:327); single/2-step/agent dispatched in `_run_passes` (lines 469–479). Unit-tested.

**AC3 — No criterion greps codebase except E4/G1G2/A1/G6.**
`CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` (registry.py:54); `test_only_code_grounding_set_greps` enforces it.

**AC5 — Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time; too-big pairing is failure finding; absence verified against COMPLETE set.**
`_run_container()` loops per child (orchestrator.py:219–256); oversized pairings emit a failure finding (lines 224–243); the complete sibling roster is fed to `pass1_container` for cross-check (passes.py:258–265). `test_container_loop_per_child_and_too_big_pairing` covers this end-to-end.

**AC6 (partial) — ISF on too-large session log falls back to documented SUMMARY with reduced confidence.**
`_linked_session_log()` summarizes oversized logs via `passes.summarize_for_isf()` (orchestrator.py:296–298); `_reduced_confidence` flag set on findings (passes.py:332); `summarized` flag recorded in coverage. Unit-tested by `test_pass1_isf_tags_findings_and_reduced_confidence`.

**AC-T8 — DET tier has explicit CODE execution path.**
`det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` invoked as first step before LLM tiers (orchestrator.py:327); `DET_CHECKS = (p1…p8)` (det_floor.py:627–636); result dispatches to blocking/advisory/coverage buckets separately from LLM findings.

---

### NOT MET

**AC4 / AC6 — Runtime context-window escalation ladder: batch → one-criterion/call → escalate model → failure finding.**

The ticket requires a runtime escalation path: when a batched call is calculated to exceed or hits a context limit, fall back to one-criterion-per-call; if still over, escalate to a higher-context model (up a configured ladder); if still over, emit a failure finding. The `MODEL_LADDER` is defined (orchestrator.py:45–49) and `largest_window_tokens()` uses it for P8's pre-run budget check only. **There is no runtime escalation code anywhere in `_run_passes`, `pass1_chunk`, or any other path**: no logic catches a context-limit error and retries with smaller batches, no logic detects that a batch is calculated to exceed the context and falls back to single-criterion calls, and no logic switches to a higher-context model mid-run. The only active use of the ladder is P8's static pre-run block. The "batch → one-per-call → escalate model" runtime path stated in AC4 and AC6 is absent from the implementation.
